subject: Human Resources
title: Sustaining Engagement Long-Term
description: Learn Sustaining Engagement Long-Term in one minute.

hook.text: A company launches a big engagement initiative. Scores spike. Six months later, they're back to baseline. Sound familiar?

buildup.text: Engagement isn't a campaign—it's a continuous discipline, like fitness. One program won't create lasting change.

discovery.text: Sustained engagement requires embedding it in daily management practices—not isolating it as an annual HR event.

twist.text: The companies with the highest engagement don't have the best programs. They have the best managers—consistently.

climax.text: Building engagement into hiring, onboarding, management training, and performance systems makes it self-sustaining.

punchline.text: Engagement isn't a project with an end date. It's how you operate.

quiz.question: Why do most engagement initiatives fail to last?
options: They're treated as one-time programs instead of continuous practice
Employees don't want to be engaged
